title: Build-time inline config.example.yaml as the workspace DEFAULT_CONFIG (anti-drift)
description:
Follow-up to bd-799000 (root-fix). The workspace's DEFAULT_CONFIG in site/workspace/workspace.js is a HAND-MAINTAINED copy of config.example.yaml's operators — it drifted (bd-799000: it was in the wrong schema + missing operators, so the ops list broke). The durable fix is to stop hand-maintaining it: inline the real config.example.yaml at build time.

Proposal (aur-1's build lane, they offered to take it): in scripts/build-docs.sh and/or .github/workflows/pages.yml (P7), which already `cp config.example.yaml site/workspace/config.example.yaml`, additionally generate the workspace default from it — e.g. write a small `site/workspace/default-config.js` setting `window.__NLIR_DEFAULT_CONFIG__ = <config.example.yaml text>`, and have workspace.js use `DEFAULT_CONFIG = window.__NLIR_DEFAULT_CONFIG__ || <minimal fallback>`. Then DEFAULT_CONFIG can never drift from the canonical config again.

Bonus (aur-1 flagged): also fixes site/hero-live.html — it fetches workspace/config.example.yaml and falls back to a config-free keystone if it 404s; a build-inlined config makes the hero always-current + never fall back.

Acceptance: (1) workspace DEFAULT_CONFIG is generated from config.example.yaml at build (no hand-maintained copy), (2) the ops list shows the full operator set with zero drift risk, (3) hero uses the inlined config. Assign: aur-1 (build/site lane). Interim: bd-799000 landed a correct hand-written complete config, so the workspace is not broken while this lands.

title: nlir dual-game: golf-aur2-kilobyte.sh (2^10) + target-aur2-50-palindrome.sh (:simplify linguistics→plain)
description:
nlir dual-game tick (aur-2). Two examples:

FORWARD golf-aur2-kilobyte.sh — "the kilobyte": 'two'**'ten' = 2^10 = 1024. Why a kilobyte isn't quite a thousand bytes: coercion reads the worded numbers + the (right-assoc) power operator raises 2 to the tenth = 1024. CS trivia from two words. Verified -> 1024.

Like the word 'level'... Or the phrase 'never odd or even'..." Closeness: same definition + both examples kept, plain (high), ~even length.

title: <text> tag leak: browser realiser bypasses strip_text_tags; wrappers accumulate over nested LLM ops
description:
Reported by Harry (browser wasm): nested `!(!(!(!(!(~ …)))))` accumulates `<text>…</text>` delimiter wrapper pairs — one added per LLM realisation, so by 5 ops the value is `<text><text><text><text><text>…</text></text></text></text></text>`. Negation semantics are CORRECT (each ! flips the claim); only the wrapper tags leak. Harry: "probably also in the main version."

ROOT CAUSE (two parts):
1. The browser path bypasses the strip. `strip_text_tags` (bd-b1d501) is applied inside the NATIVE `run_llm`/`extract_result`, but the browser path is `eval.rs realise_async → realiser.llm() (JsRealiser fetches base-url) → .map(Value::string)` with NO strip. The native claude CLI obeys the "omit <text> tags" system prompt; a browser BYO model may echo them, and nothing strips them → they leak and get re-wrapped by the next op's prompt delimiter, accumulating.
2. `strip_text_tags` strips only ONE outer wrapper, so a multi-wrapper echo (or an operand that already carries a leaked wrapper) leaves residue.

FIX: (a) apply the strip at the SHARED realise_async seam (realiser-agnostic — covers both the browser JsRealiser and native, idempotent with the existing run_llm strip); (b) make `strip_text_tags` LOOP to strip all nested outer `<text>…</text>` wrappers. Lane: nlir-wasm eval / realiser (msm-0).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: nlir power-move #25: the changelog :['ITEM',...] — terse notes to polished release notes (no-&=map, &=weave)
description:
nlir power-move #25 (aur-2) — "the changelog": turn terse dev notes into polished user-facing release notes — one clean line per item, in the register you choose. For changelogs, release notes, "what's new" emails, standup recaps. Uses a genuinely-distinct mechanism (per-item MAP, not a &-weave).

SHAPE: :['ITEM_1', 'ITEM_2', 'ITEM_3'] — a tone op on a list WITHOUT & MAPS over each item -> one rewritten line per item. : = friendly release notes, @ = formal changelog, ~ = terse.

KEY DISTINCTION verified (corrects a prior misread): a leading op on a list WITHOUT & MAPS (one line per item); WITH & it WEAVES into one sentence. The & is the weave.
  :['a','b','c'] -> three separate plain lines (changelog)
  :&['a','b','c'] -> one flowing sentence (summary)

Filled + REAL (claude-sonnet-5): :['fixed a crash on large uploads','sped up search 3x','added keyboard shortcuts'] ->
"Fixed a problem that made big file uploads stop working" / "Made searching much, much faster" / "Added shortcut keys you can press on the keyboard" (also verified @[...] gives a formal changelog, @&[...] weaves to one sentence)

Deliverables: examples/move-aur2-changelog.sh (runs nlir live, --examples gate exit 0), POWERMOVES.md quick-ref row (with the no-&=map / &=weave note), showcase card nlir-changelog.png (my 16th card; single --only regen, honest llm pill) + scripts/build-showcase.py row.

title: Add generation / instruction-following operator (proven config-only prototype)
description:
GAP (found dogfooding Harry's "generate agent replies AS nlir programs" directive, live model): nlir has a rich TRANSFORM category (~ gist, < shorten, @ formal, : simplify, > expand, Δ diff, ~> imply) but NO generation / instruction-following op. No op treats its operand as an instruction to execute.
- Proof: `>"Write the single word: acknowledged"` → a paragraph *about* writing one word (> elaborates the instruction, never obeys it).
- Worse: this breaks COMPOSITION of generative thoughts. `(<(>"ack")) & (<(>"counter"))` collapses to a meta-description, not two joined sentences. You can only generate by cramming everything into one prompt string — defeating "programs on thoughts" in the generative direction.

PROVEN FIX (config-only, no Rust — operator framework already supports it; prototype at /tmp/gen.yaml):
  respond:
    op: "✎"            # PLACEHOLDER sigil — team's call (sparingly, Harry greenlight)
    arity: 1
    fixity: prefix
    model: medium
    prompt: |
      Follow the instruction in <text> and return ONLY the result -- no preamble, no explanation, no restating the instruction.
      %
Results (live model):
- OBEYS not expands: `✎"Write the single word: acknowledged"` → "acknowledged"
- COMPOSES: `(✎"warm ack: $_stdin") & (✎"gentle counter: $_stdin")` → two real sentences joined by "and", each interpolating context. Generative composability restored.
- Side finding: double-quoted strings interpolate $_stdin; single-quoted are literal.

OPEN DECISIONS (msm-0/aur-1/Harry):
1. Sigil (or word-op) for the generation op.
2. Bless into core vs ship as documented opt-in config example (it's user-space either way).
3. det: stub for test coverage (trivial template "response: %" works for the det-total bar).

Owner note: filed by aur-0 (dogfood/QA lane); design + sigil are the eval/grammar owners' call. Prototype + all evidence in scratch nlir-aur0-op-audit.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-0

title: Build aarch64 release artifacts on self-hosted runner
description:
Currently, nlir release v0.1.1 only has x86_64 Linux artifacts available (nlir-0.1.1-x86_64-linux.tar.gz). Add aarch64-linux build support to the self-hosted CI/CD pipeline to generate nlir-0.1.1-aarch64-linux.tar.gz and corresponding checksum file for ARM64 platforms.

STATUS (aur-2): the aarch64-linux cross-compile matrix leg landed @33a86ca (release.yml — dtolnay stable + rustup target add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu + gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu cross linker + CARGO_TARGET linker; packages nlir-<v>-aarch64-linux.tar.gz + .sha256; isolated via fail-fast:false; native smoke skipped for the cross leg). YAML validated (yq). NOT fully closeable yet: the ARTIFACT is only produced by a release-run. Can't cross-validate on aurora (no rustup). To complete: a v*.*.* release OR `gh workflow run release.yml -f tag=v0.1.1` produces + validates it. CAVEAT: the release `test` job runs `cargo test --all`, which can flake on msm-0's intermittent parser fuzz stack-overflow — a release re-run may need a retry. Keeping in-progress pending that validation.

DE-RISK (aur-2): audited the dep tree for aarch64 cross-compile hazards — it's effectively PURE RUST, so the leg is low-risk. ureq uses rustls (features=["json"], NO openssl); Cargo.lock has ZERO openssl-sys + ZERO native-tls; resvg uses pure-Rust fontconfig-parser + tiny-skia (no C fontconfig/freetype/harfbuzz); the only -sys crates are linux-raw-sys (pure Rust syscall bindings) + windows-sys (unused on Linux). So the aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu cross-build needs ONLY the gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu linker the leg installs — no aarch64 system libs. High confidence it builds clean when a release is run (repo-admin/Harry, since I hit gh 403). Parser-fuzz flake in the test gate is also fixed (msm-0 @76cbd2d).
creator: helsinki:_:node-token

title: CLI: expression starting with '-' fails clap parsing (nlir -e '-1^*0') — needs allow_hyphen_values on --expr
description:
Dogfood finding (aur-0, while reconciling the range grammar with msm-0). An nlir expression passed to `-e` that STARTS with a `-` fails clap argument parsing instead of being treated as the expression:

  nlir -e '-1^*0'   => error: unexpected argument '-1' found
  nlir -e '-3^*-1'  => error: unexpected argument '-3' found
  nlir -e '-5'      => error: unexpected argument '-5' found

clap consumes the leading `-1`/`-3`/`-5` as an unknown flag before it reaches the expr value. This bites any expression that opens with a negative message index in a range form (e.g. `-1^*0` = a range from the last message), and any leading-negative token.

WORKAROUNDS (already functional): `nlir -e=-1^*0`, or `nlir -e -- -1^*0`, or `nlir -- -e '-1^*0'` depending on clap layout.

FIX: set `allow_hyphen_values(true)` (clap) on the `-e/--expr` arg (src/main.rs CLI definition) so a leading `-` in the expression value is not parsed as a flag. Verify it doesn't break real short flags. Consider the same for any other value-taking arg that legitimately accepts leading-`-` payloads.

Note: `-5` etc. are not valid nlir anyway (no unary-minus operator; negatives come from `0-5`), so the practical hit is mainly leading-negative-index range forms — a narrow but real CLI ergonomics paper-cut. No existing bead (searched hyphen / "unexpected argument" = 0). CLI lane = msm-0. Filed via reflect-session / dogfood from the range-grammar reconciliation.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-0

title: nlir coercion: currency ($) coerces OFFLINE in det mode too (completes the offline number set)
description:
nlir coercion follow-up (aur-2 types/coercion lane) to bd-4f1925. Currency ('$1'/'$0.25'/'$1,000') was still falling through to the LLM — errored in --mode det. Completed the offline-coercion set.

Fix (src/value.rs): added a '$'-strip branch to parse_number_str (after the plain-float attempt) — strip the leading '$' and re-parse the amount; recursion handles the inner decimals/thousands separators ('$1,000' -> '1,000' -> 1000). Backward-compatible.

Offline set is now: plain float / currency($) / hex(0x) / binary(0b) / thousands(1,000) / percent(50%) / fraction(1/2). Verified OFFLINE in det (no key): '$1'+'1'=2, '$0.25'+'$0.25'=0.5, '$1,000'+'1'=1001, '$0.50'*'2'=1. Full CI GREEN: fmt + clippy --all-targets -D warnings + test --all (special-forms test extended with currency cases) + nlir test 17/17. The CATALOG's money.sh ('$1'/'$0.25'=4) coercion is now genuinely offline, zero LLM calls.

title: Render nlir specification on GitHub Pages site
description:
Create and render the nlir (natural-language IR) specification documentation on the GitHub Pages site.

The specification should document:
- nlir as a natural-language IR system that transpiles terse, sigil-laden shorthand into fluent English
- The shorthand functioning as an intermediate representation that is tokenised and parsed into a DAG using a config-defined grammar
- Evaluation over a small stack machine with a tiny type system
- Two operator realisation modes: deterministic (mechanical string/number expansion) and LLM-based (structured text transformation via LLM call)

Acceptance Criteria:
- GitHub Pages site is configured to serve the specification
- nlir specification document is complete and accessible
- Documentation clearly explains the architecture: tokenisation → DAG parsing → stack machine evaluation → operator realisation
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: Update feedback to target new nlir feedback hook instead of omni
description:
The nlir feedback system is currently targeting an omni webhook endpoint (http://helsinki.miku-owl.ts.net:11300/hooks/global/omni-feedback) which returns 404. Update the feedback configuration to target a new nlir-specific feedback hook endpoint. This should resolve webhook delivery failures and ensure feedback is routed to the correct service.

DIAGNOSIS (aur-2, config lane): the nlir REPO code is ALREADY correct — src/lib.rs from_env routes feedback to <FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_BASE_URL>/<project> = .../nlir (its own sub-path, NOT omni); feedback_webhook_url() + its test confirm .../global/nlir. So the 404 is INFRA/DEPLOY-SIDE, not an nlir-repo PR: either (a) the deploy env exports an explicit FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_URL=.../omni-feedback that OVERRIDES the base+/nlir routing (unset it so the canonical base+/<project> applies), or (b) the /hooks/global/nlir feedback hook does not exist on the hooks server yet (create it via caco hooks config). Fix belongs in caco-hooks/deploy-env, not the nlir source tree — no repo change needed. Leaving unclaimed for whoever owns the hook/deploy config.

CONFIRMED (aur-2): `caco hooks list --project nlir` returns ZERO hooks — the `/hooks/global/nlir` feedback hook does NOT exist on the hooks server. This definitively confirms the earlier diagnosis (option b): the nlir repo feedback code is already correct (from_env routes to <FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_BASE_URL>/nlir); the 404 is because the target hook was never created. RESOLUTION (infra/deploy owner, NOT a nlir-repo PR): create the nlir feedback webhook in the cacophony cluster/node config (the webhooks are declared in cacophony config, not the nlir repo, and there's no `caco hooks create` CLI — it's config-defined). No action possible from the nlir checkout or via caco CLI by this agent. Ready for whoever owns the hooks/deploy config to create the hook.

ROUTING (msm-1, 2026-07-07 queue hygiene): labeling operator-action. Per aur-2's confirmed diagnosis this is NOT a nlir-repo change and cannot be actioned from a nlir worker checkout or via caco CLI — it requires creating the /hooks/global/nlir webhook in the cacophony cluster/deploy config (operator/infra owner; the bd-703f05 guardrail forbids workers editing canonical cacophony config directly). Marked operator-action so it stops re-surfacing in the worker --ready queue and inviting duplicate re-diagnosis. No nlir-side work remains.

description:
Parent epic bd-b71b0b (label `llm`). Add a single high-level LLM-mode realisation helper in src/llm.rs so eval::realise()'s Mode::Llm arm (currently Unsupported) is a one-call wire on the eval.rs side (aur-1 owns eval; aur-2 owns this llm.rs helper — agreed 2026-07-03).

Signature (confirmed with aur-1):
  realise_llm(model_alias: Option<&str>, prompt_template: &str, operands: &[String], config: &Config, cli_model: Option<&str>, env_lookup: impl Fn(&str)->Option<String>) -> Result<String, RealiseError>

Composes the landed pieces: resolve_model (op alias -> cli_model -> defaults.model precedence) + substitute_operands (fill %) + resolve_prompt_fragments + assemble_nlir_vars, then dispatch on model.kind — anthropic via run_anthropic_backend (substitute_nlir_vars over messages), command via run_command_backend with the NLIR_ARGS bash-array declaration prepended so command operators can index ${NLIR_ARGS[k]}. Returns the raw extracted result String (caller wraps in Value::string). RealiseError { Model | Command | Anthropic } with Display+Error. Filed via the eval↔llm coordination.

title: [bug] ** (pow) is left-associative — 2**3**2 = 64, should be 512 (right-assoc per SPEC "normal math")
description:
Found dogfooding in det mode (aur-0, no network). Exponentiation `**` parses as LEFT-associative, but mathematical convention (and Python) make it RIGHT-associative, and the SPEC says "binary follows normal math — ** > * / > + -".

Reproduction (all via `nlir --config config.example.yaml --mode det --quiet`):
- `2**3**2` => 64   (left: (2**3)**2 = 8**2 = 64; expected right: 2**(3**2) = 2**9 = 512)
- `2**1**3` => 8    (left: (2**1)**3 = 8; expected right: 2**(1**3) = 2)
- `4**3**0` => 1    (left: (4**3)**0 = 1; expected right: 4**(3**0) = 4)

Correct for comparison (left-assoc IS right for these): `10-4-3 => 3`, `16/4/2 => 2` — subtraction and division are correctly left-associative.

Root cause: src/parser.rs treats ALL binary/infix/mixfix operators as uniformly left-associative. See the module doc comment (lines ~10-12): "infix (`- /`) is binary, left-associative; mixfix (`& | + *`) is treated as left-associative binary here". `pow` (`op: **`, fixity: infix, arity: 2) inherits this uniform left-assoc, so chained `**` groups left instead of right. The binding-power helper already notes it doubles priority "so left-associativity has room" — i.e. right-assoc is a (right-binding-power - 1) adjustment away.

Proposed fix (config-idiomatic, keeps "the language is config"):
1. Add an optional `assoc: left|right` field to the operator config struct in src/config.rs (default `left`; validate the enum).
2. Set `assoc: right` on the `pow` operator in config.example.yaml (and the SPEC's example config).
3. In src/parser.rs Pratt loop: for a right-associative infix op at priority p, recurse the right operand with min binding power (2p - 1) instead of 2p, so equal-precedence continues rightward.
4. SPEC.md: add an explicit associativity note (binary ops left-associative by default; `**` right-associative), since the Grammar/precedence section currently omits associativity.
5. Tests: parser unit test for `2**3**2` right-grouping; a config `tests:` case `pow-right-assoc: { mode: det, expr: "2**3**2", expected: "512" }`.

Cross-lane note: parser = msm-0 lane, config schema = aur-2 lane. Filed by aur-0; will coordinate before landing. Low frequency in practice (chained ** is rare) but a clear spec-conformance gap. No existing bead (searched associativ/exponent = 0).
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-0

title: showcase: fuzzy-count (#2) + fuzzy-decide cards — round out the "why nlir" set (filter→count, decision)
description:
Two more referee-confirmed "why nlir" cards, both explicitly pulled to msm-2's site-card lane:

1. fuzzy-count (msm-0's #2, greenlit): `{$0+$1}⊘({$0~>'a complaint'}↦['love it','it crashed on save','billing charged me twice'])` → 2. The fuzzy-FILTER→exact-COUNT complement to fuzzy-sum: grep can't judge tone, a model can't be trusted to count. Verified: det → 0 (keyword ~> matches none), llm → 2.

2. fuzzy-decide (aur-2's offer, landed @ad34969, aur-0-gated): `$if%('the server keeps crashing'~>'urgent','escalate','queue')` → escalate. NEW category (decision/routing), rounding out access·generation·pipe·decision. Killer hook: the SAME expression prints "queue" in det (exact keyword-match — no literal "urgent") vs "escalate" in llm (semantic — crashing IS urgent). The gap between the two IS what fuzzy ~> buys. Verified: det → queue, llm → escalate.

Deliverable: 2 build-showcase.py SIMPLE cards (fuzzy-count pill "llm+det · fuzzy filter → exact count" out 2; fuzzy-decide pill "llm · fuzzy decision" out escalate), render PNGs, add fuzzy-decide (the decision category, best divergence hook) to the README "Why nlir, not a prompt?" block and pair fuzzy-count with fuzzy-sum. Credits msm-0 (#2), aur-2 (decide), aur-0 (referee). No new example scripts — msm-0 owns move-msm0-pipe.sh, aur-2 owns move-aur2-decide.sh.

Note (bd-3a589a): both fuzzy-count and fuzzy-decide are prime candidates to become CI-exact-gated once `_seed` + aur-0's backend honor-matrix name a reproducing backend.

title: Leading-dot float literals (.5) do not lex — decide if .5 should parse as 0.5
description:
Reflect-session dogfood observation (aur-0, from the bd-df62f1 session). After bd-f551f9 (float literals) was fixed, bare floats with an integer part lex fine (0.5, 3.14, 2**0.5). Two remaining decimal-point cases still error:
- `.5` (leading dot, no integer part) => lex error at position 0: unexpected character '.'
- `3.` (trailing dot) => lex error at position 1

`3.` and `1.2.3` (double dot) rejections are INTENTIONAL per the bd-f551f9 close note ("reject a bare trailing/double dot"). The only arguable gap is leading-dot `.5`, which many languages accept as 0.5. This is a minor convenience, and may well be intentionally excluded too — filing as a low-pri draft so lexer-lane triage (msm-0) can decide whether `.5` should lex as 0.5 or stay rejected. Not blocking anything; `0.5` already works.

Lexer lane = msm-0. Filed via reflect-session from bd-df62f1 session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-0

title: nlir help: add comparison + conditional examples ($if on ==/<=/>=, the count idiom) — bd-5c19c4 follow-up part 2
description:
Follow-up part 2 to bd-5c19c4 / bd-502b6c (nlir help learning resource). Comparison ops == != <= >= landed @2521bf1 (aur-2; deterministic, result: bool), unblocking the deferred conditional flagship. Add a "comparison → predicate + conditional" examples group to `nlir help` (print_help_examples in src/main.rs):
- comparisons → bool: 4==4→true, 3<=5→true, 5>=9→false, 'a'!='b'→true
- $if on a real comparison: $if%(3<=5,'yes','no')→yes, $if%(4==4,'match','differ')→match
- the COUNT flagship (aur-2/aur-0 correctness-gate): $fold%({$0+$1},$map%({$0>=5},[3,7,2,9]))→2 (count how many pass a predicate) — comparison → map → Bool→Number → fold, the whole composable control-flow backbone in one line.

Every example verified-runnable + a nlir test case (aur-2 added 7 comparison det tests @2521bf1; add help-* keys for any gap). Consistent with bd-5c19c4/bd-502b6c pattern (help ≡ nlir test). Note: strict </> are view ops (question/expand), so comparisons are <=/>=/==/!= only.

title: nlir power-move #27: the templated message NAME='V'; @&["...$NAME..."] — bind once, reuse across the message
description:
nlir power-move #27 (aur-2) — "the templated message": bind a value ONCE, reuse it across a whole message; change it in one place and every mention updates. Parameterized comms / a reusable template. A genuinely-fresh move on nlir string interpolation (nobody had built one; distinct from aur-0's reflect-lane = binding, which is for self-reflection).

SHAPE: NAME='VALUE'; @&["... $NAME ...", "... $NAME ...", ...] — bind once, reference $NAME in DOUBLE-quoted slots; the composer weaves + fills it in. THE RULE (verified): interpolation needs DOUBLE quotes — "$NAME" fills the value, '$NAME' stays literal.

Filled + REAL (claude-sonnet-5): svc='the auth service';@&["$svc moves to us-east this friday","expect a ten-minute maintenance window for $svc","roll back $svc if error rates exceed two percent"] -> "The authentication service will be migrated to US-East this Friday. A ten-minute maintenance window should be expected, and the service will be rolled back if error rates exceed two percent." (all three $svc filled; weave dedups the repeated subject naturally).

Deliverables: examples/move-aur2-template.sh (runs nlir live + interpolates, --examples gate exit 0), POWERMOVES.md quick-ref row, showcase card nlir-templated-message.png (my 21st card; single --only regen, honest llm pill) + scripts/build-showcase.py row.

title: parallelism CLI flag resolved then dropped: override never reaches the scheduler
description:
Follow-up to bd-149949 (msm-0's note: "--parallelism 1 did not force sequential in the sleep test — flag->scheduler wiring gap"). Confirmed + fixed.

Root cause: run_eval / run_repl call resolve_defaults(&cfg, &cli_overrides(cli)) which DOES resolve the CLI parallelism override into settings.parallelism, but only settings.mode is passed to evaluate(). The Evaluator reads its parallelism from config.defaults.parallelism (the frozen evaluate(expr,&Config,&mut Context,Mode) signature has no parallelism param), so settings.parallelism is computed then silently dropped -- the CLI flag had no effect.

Fix (main.rs resolve_config, the single choke point all eval paths share): after loading, apply cfg.defaults.parallelism = cli.parallelism.max(1) when set, so the override reaches the scheduler via the config the Evaluator already reads. Respects the frozen evaluate() signature (no API change).

Verified with a sleep-2s command operator, ~a&~b&~c: default(8)=2s (concurrent), parallelism 1=6s (sequential), parallelism 2=4s (chunked 2+1). The parallelism=1->sequential eval behaviour is already locked by the det_seq cache test. Found+fixed by aur-1 while driving nlir in llm mode.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: nlir composer move: the graceful decline (:&["CANT","WHY","CAN_DO"]) — #41
description:
nlir power-moves tick (aur-2) — fresh COMPOSER move #41 (migration-proof "..." slots; holding on quote-eval greenlight; session revived post-crash, board clean). "the graceful decline": say no while keeping the relationship — the honest can't, the real why, and the door you leave open.

SHAPE: `:&["I_CANT_DO_X", "THE_HONEST_WHY", "BUT_HERES_WHAT_I_CAN"]`. The `:` leading tone makes it WARM (formal @ would read like an HR rejection); the weave braids the no + reason + alternative so you decline without stinging. Distinct from descope-proposal (#32, trims a PROJECT's scope) and disagree-and-commit (#40, yields to a decision).

Real (claude-sonnet-5): :&["I cannot take on the security audit this sprint", "my plate is full with the release", "but I can review your threat model doc on Thursday"] -> "I can't do the whole security check this sprint because I'm busy getting the release ready. But I can look at your threat model paper on Thursday."

Files (mine only): examples/move-aur2-decline.sh (SHAPE+slots+filled+real output; runs live, exit 0) + examples/POWERMOVES.md (row). No new card — composer pattern already carded.

title: Lexer: `_` echo operator can't tokenise (lex_bare consumes `_` mid-token)
description:
The SPEC `_` echo operator (op: "_", the det-echo test `xxx_2` → "xxx xxx") cannot be tokenised. lex_bare (src/lexer.rs ~line 369) treats `_` as a bare-token continuation char (`c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_'`) — a consequence of allowing `_`-prefixed system keys like `_sep`. So `xxx_2` lexes as a single Bare("xxx_2") and the configured `_` operator never splits it; the evaluator returns the input unchanged.

Repro: with a config operator `echo: { op: "_", arity: 2, fixity: infix, command: ... }`, `nlir -e 'xxx_2'` (det) yields "xxx_2" instead of "xxx xxx".

This blocks SPEC det-echo. The fix is in the lexer lane (msm-0): disambiguate the `_` echo operator from `_`-in-bare-token / `_`-prefixed keys — e.g. only treat leading `_` as a bare/key start (not a mid-token continuation), or let a configured operator sigil `_` win when it appears between operands, while `_name` at a key position still lexes as a key. Command realisation itself (bd-3c1e6d) is verified working via a non-`_` sigil in src/eval.rs tests; only the `_`-sigil lexing is blocked. Filed by aur-1 from the eval SPEC-completion batch (bd-c85dee/bd-02a795/bd-3c1e6d).
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1
spoken_name: lexer underscore echo operator tokenization bug

title: Boolean-AND operator (`&&`/∧) distinct from compose-`&` — clean mutual-implication judge gate
description:
Surfaced by playing the nlir-golf judge (Harry's "the judge is nlir" — 2026-07-07). The honor-system golf judge wants a MUTUAL implication gate: "does the solution mean the target, both ways?" The natural spelling `(sol~>target) & (target~>sol)` DOES NOT WORK as a boolean AND, because `&` is the string COMPOSE/"and" op (mixfix, string result) — it renders "(true) and (true)" (llm) or errors under det (`~> has no deterministic realisation` when nested under `&`). Independently confirmed live by msm-2, msm-0, aur-0.

The team's working fix is the `$if` short-circuit form:
  `$if%((sol)~>target, target~>(sol), 'false')`  → clean "true"/"false", and only ONE llm call when the first implication already fails.

GAP: nlir has no boolean-AND operator distinct from compose-`&`. A first-class logical conjunction (`&&` or ∧) — deterministic, `operands: bool`, `result: bool` — would let the mutual judge (and any bool composition) read cleanly `(A)&&(B)` instead of the `$if` workaround. Sibling to the landed comparison ops (== != <= >=) and set ops (∪ ∩ ∖ ∈). Likely also want boolean-OR (`||`/∨) and maybe NOT-on-bool for completeness.

COORDINATE: touches the operator table + SPEC + verify-spec-ops (aur-1's lane) and possibly a `reduce`/builtin bool-and in the engine (msm-1's set/builtin lane). Filed as a draft by msm-2 (site-card/example lane) from the golf session; endorsed as a candidate by msm-0. aur-1/msm-1 to own if promoted. Until then the `$if` form is the documented judge gate.

Filed via golf-session observation from bd-e097c1 / the nlir-as-judge work.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-2

The signature det+fuzzy MIX: the model fuzzy-extracts each count from the messy strings (det coercion errors on "3 apples"→number — proving the fuzzy half genuinely needs the model), while the EXACT `+` sums them (a raw prompt can't be trusted to add). No single unix tool does "sum these fuzzily-worded lines." title: Parser: stack overflow on deeply-nested input (fuzz-found) — add recursion depth guard
description:
A fuzz test (no-panic corpus over tokenize+parse) found that the recursive-descent Pratt parser has NO recursion-depth bound, so deeply-nested input (e.g. `nlir -e '(((((…a…)))))'` ~256+ deep, or nested `[a,[a,…`) recurses until the stack overflows → SIGABRT (exit 134), crashing the CLI instead of returning a clean error. DoS-class robustness bug.

Fix: added `MAX_PARSE_DEPTH = 256` and a depth guard on the `expr` recursion chokepoint (Parser gains a `depth` field; `expr` increments/checks/decrements around the renamed `expr_inner`). All recursion (groups `(`, lists `[`, prefix ops, backtick, message index) routes through the guarded `expr`, so deeper nesting now returns `parse error: expression nesting too deep` instead of overflowing. Verified: depth 100 → ok, depth 256/300/2000 → clean error (exit 1). 256 is safely below both the ~2MB test-thread and 8MB main-thread overflow points. Added `fuzz_tokenize_and_parse_never_panic` (deterministic xorshift corpus, no proptest dep) to lock the no-panic guarantee. Filed + fixed by msm-0.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: Bare float literals do not lex: decimal point rejected (3.14, 0.5, 2**0.5)
description:
Found while driving nlir in LLM mode. Bare decimal/float number literals fail to lex:
- `nlir -e '3.14'` => lex error at position 1: unexpected character '.'
- `nlir -e '0.5'` => same
- `nlir -e '2**0.5'` => lex error on the '.'
- `nlir -e '1.5+2.5'` => same

But float RESULTS work fine (`7/2` => 3.5) and QUOTED decimals coerce (`'3.14'+'2.86'` => 6). So you can COMPUTE floats but cannot WRITE them as literals — an asymmetry confirming this is a gap, not intended integer-only.

Root: lexer.rs emits numbers as `Token::Bare(digit-run)` (as_number accepts `s.bytes().all(is_ascii_digit)`), and the standalone number scanner does not consume a `.`+fractional part, so `.` hits the "unexpected character / not a configured sigil" path. Fix: extend the bare-number lexing to accept an optional single `.` + fractional digits (and reject a bare trailing/double dot), producing a Token::Number(f64) or a float-parseable Bare. Watch the interaction with `^N.M`? (no — ranges are M^N, not dotted) and with `_sep`/list commas. Lexer lane = msm-0. Filed by aur-1.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

Semantics the store already implements (strict first-present-wins): `--context-file` > session import > `NLIR_CONTEXT` env > default file. Write-through targets: `--context-file` and the default file are file-backed; `NLIR_CONTEXT` env and session import are TRANSIENT (in-memory only, no write-through). Session-file *format parsing* is NOT this bead nor the store — it belongs to the sessions epic (bd-720cdb parse → Map, bd-000666 merge); that Map is handed to `LoadSources.session`. `default_context_path(&cfg, HOME)` + `expand_tilde` resolve `context.file_default` (tilde-expanded). SPEC clarity gap surfaced while implementing: the SPEC §CLI list writes "--session-file merge" which could read as merge-on-top-of-lower-precedence rather than strict-precedence; the store chose strict first-present-wins — confirm that's the intended reading when wiring this.

title: Operator realisation kinds: `form:` and `builtin:` — bind glyphs to forms + builtins (map/fold) as an alt to command/prompt
description:
Operator request (Harry, 2026-07-05, via sgu24-app): "do we have glyphs for map and fold? would be useful to be able to assign glyphs to builtins in the config, or to forms, as an alt to command or prompt mode."

Today map/fold are $map/$fold (named builtins via %), no glyph (deliberately glyph-free to conserve sigils). This bead: add TWO new operator REALISATION KINDS to the config, alongside reduce/template/join/command/prompt, so any glyph can be bound to a form or a builtin:

1. `form:` — an operator realised by applying a FORM to its operands.
   e.g. `steelman: { op: "⇑", arity: 1, fixity: prefix, form: "{~(>@$0)}" }` → `⇑x` = summarise(expand(formalise(x))).
   Desugars an application to `{form}%(operands)` — binds operand values to $0..$N-1 in the form and evaluates. Promotes the named-macro library into first-class glyph-operators (user-extensible vocabulary, no prompts/commands). The form source is parsed once at config-load (reuse parse_stored_form / the config-grammar reparse).

2. `builtin:` — bind a glyph to a builtin like map/fold.
   e.g. `mapop: { op: "↦", arity: 2, fixity: infix, builtin: map }`, `foldop: { op: "⊘", arity: 2, fixity: infix, builtin: fold }` → `{$0*$0}↦[1,2,3]` maps.
   Dispatches to the existing eval_higher_order (map/fold engine, bd-14af74). Operands eval operand-first (form operand → Value::Form, list → Value::List) → higher-order.

LANE: config schema (OperatorConfig: add `form`/`builtin` fields) + eval realisation dispatch (realise/realise_async: check builtin/form kinds) = msm-0 (eval). Adds glyphs (grammar) → coordinate sigil/schema with aur-1 (sigil) + aur-2 (config). Harry greenlit by request.

Design: additive. realise() gains two branches: builtin → eval_higher_order over the (pre-evaluated) operands; form → build FormApply{form: parsed, args: operands} and eval. Operand-first eval already gives the form/list values. Tests: `⇑`-style form-operator + `↦`/`⊘` map/fold-operator in a test config; nlir test cases. Open Q for Harry: ship example glyph-operators in config.example.yaml (↦/⊘/⇑) or leave glyph-choice entirely to user config.

---

Duplicate of bd-44c294
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: config: haiku (number/bool coercion) base_url missing /v1 → anthropic 404 blocks det+llm-mix examples
description:
config.example.yaml: the `haiku` model (used by `types.number.model` + `types.bool.model` — every llm-mode number/bool coercion) is `type: anthropic_messages` with `base_url: $ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, MISSING the `/v1` its sibling `direct` has (`base_url: ${LITELLM_BASE_URL}/v1`). The anthropic backend appends `/messages` (src/llm.rs:584), so haiku posts to `$ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/messages` → 404 (the proxy serves /v1/messages), blocking every number/bool coercion — i.e. the flagship det+llm-mix examples: `$fold%({$0+$1},['3 apples','5 oranges','2 pears'])`→10 and `$fold%({$0+$1},['yes','no','yes','yes'])` tally. Ops routing to sonnet/claude-CLI (~, @) are unaffected.

Not a code/path bug (confirmed by msm-0): nlir intentionally appends only `/messages`, so base_url must include the version segment — the standard Anthropic convention (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL = host, endpoint = /v1/messages). `direct` follows it; `haiku` doesn't.

Independent confirmation: ms-mac (msm-3) probe — /messages→404, /v1/messages→401(exists); aurora (aur-0) 404s identically + repro-proved apples=10 when coercion routes correctly. WASM playground unaffected (aur-1: uses OpenAI /chat/completions).

FIX (this bead, msm-0-approved): one-line config.example.yaml — `haiku.base_url: ${ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL}/v1` (align with direct) + a comment clarifying the convention (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL = host, no /v1; nlir appends /v1/messages via config-/v1 + code-/messages). Safe because haiku already 404s so no working deploy relies on the old behavior; correct-by-inspection (full apples=10 verify pending proxy DB-up + key — aur-0 to co-verify).

FOLLOW-UP (separate, aur-2's call, NOT in this bead): routing types.number/bool → the command backend (sonnet) would drop the anthropic_messages proxy SPOF entirely, but loses the json_schema structured-number guarantee (command returns parsed text). Flagged for aur-2.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-3

title: nlir power-move #19: the question set ['ASSUMPTION'?,...] — flip assumptions into the questions to ask (fresh ? operator)
description:
nlir power-move #19 (aur-2) — "the question set": turn your nagging assumptions into the pointed questions to ask BEFORE committing. A genuinely-fresh move on a previously-UNTOUCHED operator: ? (postfix) flips a statement into a question. Nobody in the swarm had built a ?-based move.

SHAPE: ['ASSUMPTION_1'?, 'ASSUMPTION_2'?, ...] — a list; ? is POSTFIX (goes AFTER each string; [?'x'] is a parse error "? not valid in prefix position"). Each ? rewrites the statement as the well-formed question that tests it, one per line. Lead with @ to formalize each question, : to keep them plain.

Real intent: you think in assumptions, the people you need answers from think in questions — ? does the flip. Due-diligence / kickoff / code-review / risk-assessment checklist.

Filled + REAL (claude-sonnet-5): ['the timeline is fixed'?,'the budget covers a full rewrite'?,'the team has the bandwidth'?] ->
"Is the timeline fixed?" / "Does the budget cover a full rewrite?" / "Does the team have the bandwidth?" (and @[...] formalizes: "Does the team have sufficient bandwidth to undertake this effort?")

Deliverables: examples/move-aur2-questions.sh (runs nlir live, --examples gate exit 0), POWERMOVES.md quick-ref row, showcase card nlir-question-set.png (my 9th card; single --only regen, honest llm pill) + scripts/build-showcase.py row. Fresh operator (?), distinct from all swarm moves.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-2

title: $filter / where builtin — the select of the map/filter/fold trinity
description:
Gap surfaced by the composable-core loop (Harry's APL/J refinement, docs/design/composable-core.md §3.2). We have `$map` (transform-each) and `$fold` (reduce) but NO select — the missing third of the map/filter/fold trinity. aur-0's gap-ranking has this at #2 (highest leverage-per-cost; a deterministic builtin twin of `$map`).

## Spec
`$filter%({pred}, xs)` — keep `xs[i]` where `pred(xs[i])` is truthy → a List.
- `pred` is a form returning a Bool, or a value coerced to Bool via the existing deterministic Bool coercion (nonzero number / non-empty / "yes"-ish).
- Deterministic builtin, twin of the `map` builtin — same dispatch site (the `builtin:` / map-fold engine msm-0 owns).
- Empty result → empty list; preserves order.
- det example (once comparison ops land): `$filter%({$0 > 2}, [1,2,3,4])` → `[3,4]`.
- det example today (bool list): `$filter%({$0}, [1,0,1])` → `[1,1]`.
- mixed text+det: `$filter%({$0 ~> 'urgent'}, msgs)` → the urgent subset (fuzzy per-item classify, det select structure).

## Why
Completes the functional trinity so programs can SELECT, not just transform/count. Unlocks the "urgent digest" target program (composable-core.md §2): `$fold%({~($0&$1)}, $map%({~$0}, $filter%({$0~>'urgent'}, msgs)))`. Verified today: `$filter` errors ("unknown context key `filter`").

## Lane
Impl = aur-2 (config/builtin registration) + msm-0 (eval/map-fold engine dispatch) — the same seam as $map/$fold and the bd-44c294 builtin: ops. Spec filed by msm-2 from the composable-core loop; NOT claimed (impl is the eval/config lane). aur-0 has verify cases ready. Pairs with $scan (#3) as the next adverb.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-2

title: nlir-wasm: regression test guarding the config-as-JSON boundary (Config round-trips through JSON)
description: nlir-wasm epic bd-360d0c prep/de-risk (aur-0). aur-1's design has the nlir-wasm crate take config AS JSON at the wasm boundary (JS does YAML→JSON via js-yaml), then serde_json::from_str::<Config>, keeping msm-0's P0 entry format-agnostic (&Config). This adds a regression test (config.rs) that guards that boundary: loads the example config, round-trips it Config→JSON→Config (assert_eq), and evaluates a det expr over the JSON-loaded config. Fails loudly if anyone adds a serde_yaml-only field to Config (which would silently break the wasm path). Confirms empirically that Config is serde-format-agnostic (derives Serialize/Deserialize/PartialEq; dynamic fields are serde_json::Value; serde_yaml lives only in the native loader). Green: 215 lib tests, clippy -D, fmt.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-0

bead.title: Stream each eval step live as it arrives (CLI/TUI/WASM), not batch at the end
bead.description:
Harry (2026-07-05): "in the CLI, TUI, WASM builds, can we show each step live as it comes in, not just dump all steps at the end?"

Today the step surfaces BATCH: CLI run_step (main.rs) calls eval::step_trace() which returns Vec<String> (all steps computed), then loops + prints. Same batch pattern in the TUI step-through (run_step_view / the Ctrl-T workbench view) and the wasm step()/graphFrames exports. In DET mode steps compute instantly so batching is invisible, but in LLM mode each step is a slow realisation — so streaming each step as it completes is a big UX win (watch it unfold, not wait for all then dump).

Proposal: a STREAMING step API in eval.rs (msm-0's eval-core lane) — e.g. step_trace_streaming(expr, cfg, ctx, mode, on_step: FnMut(&str)) (+ an async twin step_trace_streaming_async for llm), yielding each reduced expr as it's produced. Then the surfaces consume it live:
- CLI (main.rs run_step): print each step as the callback fires (flush stdout).
- TUI (run_step_view / Ctrl-T workbench): push each step into the view + requestRender as it arrives.
- WASM (crates/nlir-wasm step/graphFrames): stream via a JS callback per step (the workspace step()/animate() already render incrementally; wire the async per-step callback).

Lane split: eval-core streaming API = msm-0 (eval). Surface wiring: CLI = msm-1 (offered), TUI = msm-2, WASM = aurora. Coordinate the callback signature first, then wire each surface.

Acceptance: `nlir step` (and the TUI/workbench + wasm) print/render each step the moment it's realised, especially visible in llm mode; det behaviour unchanged.
bead.creator: ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-1
